structData.title: Audience segment upload failures — SHA-256 hash format errors
structData.content:
Audience segment uploads fail silently when the hash format is incorrect. The segment shows a size of 0 or a very low match rate even though thousands of records were submitted. The platform requires SHA-256 hashed identifiers — not plaintext emails, not MD5 hashes.

HOW TO DIAGNOSE
Call getSegmentStatus and check:
  processingState: FAILED  — upload was rejected
  size: 0                  — no users matched
  matchRate: 0%            — nothing processed

Then call getSegmentUploadLog and check:
  rejectionReason: INVALID_HASH_FORMAT  — wrong hash type
  detail field — will specify if hashes are wrong length (MD5=32 chars vs SHA-256=64 chars)

SHA-256 REQUIREMENTS
All email addresses and phone numbers must be:
1. Lowercased: JOHN@EXAMPLE.COM must become john@example.com
2. Trimmed: remove leading/trailing whitespace
3. Hashed with SHA-256 algorithm (not MD5, not SHA-1)
4. Encoded as lowercase hex string — 64 characters exactly

Example in Python:
  import hashlib
  email = 'john@example.com'
  hashed = hashlib.sha256(email.lower().strip().encode()).hexdigest()
  # Result: 64 lowercase hex characters

Example in Java:
  MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance('SHA-256');
  byte[] hash = digest.digest(email.toLowerCase().trim().getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
  String hex = HexFormat.of().formatHex(hash); // 64 chars

COMMON MISTAKES
1. MD5 hashes submitted instead of SHA-256. MD5 produces 32 hex chars, SHA-256 produces 64 hex chars. Check hash length in upload log.
2. Mixed case — hashing JOHN@EXAMPLE.COM gives a different hash than john@example.com.
3. Extra whitespace — hashing 'john@example.com ' (note trailing space) gives a different hash.
4. Double hashing — hashing an already-hashed value.
5. Base64 encoding instead of hex encoding.

MINIMUM AUDIENCE SIZE
A segment must have at least 1000 matched users to be eligible for targeting. Segments below this threshold will show processingState=PROCESSED but audienceSize too small for targeting.

AFTER FIXING
Re-upload the corrected file. Processing takes 2–4 hours for large segments. The audienceSize field will update once processing is complete.
